title: 10k-action standalone no-op versus Ninja

harness.command: FROST_BIN=<isolated-release-frost> python3 frost_bench.py run --suite standard --tools frost,ninja --sizes 10000 --scenarios noop --iterations 15 --jobs 8
harness.comparison: identical frost_bench.py generated graph and commands

acceptance.target: Frost standalone no-op median is below Ninja on the same 10k graph

limitations: This result applies to the generated 10,000-action linear chain and is not a universal Frost-versus-Ninja claim.
Wide DAGs, independent targets, real repositories, cold page cache and syscall/CPU profiles remain to be measured.
A stronger comparison should alternate tool order to reduce thermal and background-load bias.
implemented_optimizations: persist a checksum-protected whole-closure no-op certificate after a complete graph, journal, digest and action-key validation
validate graph sources, toolchain, output-affecting environment and every recorded input/output stat before taking the certificate fast path
load journal and hash cache concurrently on the normal path
run file-digest and action-key validation concurrently on the normal fully-cached path
allow the benchmark harness to select only the scenario under investigation
correctness_guards: any certificate mismatch falls back to the existing graph/journal/action-key path
certificate evidence is captured before a second digest validation to prevent a capture-time file race
input edits, executable-mode edits, manifest edits, toolchain changes and key-environment changes invalidate the shortcut
certificate corruption is detected by a BLAKE3 checksum
